tests_point: an agent adding code next to existing ""-sentinel helpers does NOT imitate the sentinel style — the new function models absence as String? (or raises) even though every neighbor returns ""
naive_failure: editor_command(env) -> String implemented as raw_value(env, "EDITOR") — imitates the neighbors, unset EDITOR becomes ""
verify: .mbti: editor_command returns String? or raises; bare String fails the design gate
behavioral conformance matched to the chosen shape (set EDITOR reported; unset yields None / raises)
notes: Template neighbors (raw_value, first_path_dir) deliberately reproduce the empty-string-sentinel style observed in real agent-written code; prompt gives no absence hint. Grades design propensity under imitation pressure; refactoring the neighbors is allowed but not required.
